skill.name: longbridge-value-investing
skill.description: Analyzes value investing opportunities with Graham NCAV and Buffett moat frameworks. It guides Longbridge CLI data collection, reconciliation checks, scoring, screening, and source-backed reports.
skill.summary: Run Graham and Buffett value investing analysis with Longbridge data.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review shows most are Markdown code spans, CLI examples, formulas, and prose rather than executable Ruby or cryptographic code. The skill is prompt-only and no prompt-injection attempt was found, but it does instruct agents to run Longbridge CLI commands and limited WebSearch, so publication should include an external-command warning.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands for quotes, financial statements, constituents, ownership, dividends, and related data. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it means generated workflows may execute local commands with ticker or index parameters supplied by users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly direct use of Longbridge CLI commands, so the external-command risk factor is real. Confidence is not higher because commands are documented examples with placeholders, not executable bundled scripts.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Detections Are Markdown
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick findings occur in Markdown inline code, fenced command examples, tables, and file layout text. No Ruby files, shell scripts, or executable backtick expressions were found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant locations are Markdown documentation and command examples, not source code that executes automatically. The repository contains only SKILL.md and reference Markdown files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Prose And Formulas
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts map to ordinary investment-methodology text, formulas, and headings. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, custom encryption, password hashing, or cryptographic API use in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines found investment language and reconciliation formulas rather than cryptographic operations. The confidence is high because the skill has no executable implementation files.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Detection Is Data-Schema Language
low_findings.description: The low-severity reconnaissance alerts correspond to references to balance-sheet cohorts and share-count reconciliation, not host or network discovery. No evidence found of system enumeration commands such as uname, whoami, ipconfig, netstat, or environment dumping.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss financial-statement categories and market-cap reconciliation. They do not instruct collection of host, user, process, or network configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt-Driven Local CLI Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to call Longbridge CLI commands and verify CLI flags before use. Marketplace users should understand that this can trigger local command execution when the assistant follows the workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions consistently direct CLI use across all workflows. The pattern is bounded to the Longbridge command examples and does not show malicious command chaining.

content.user_title: Analyze Value Stocks with Graham and Buffett
content.value_statement: Value investors need consistent screening, source checks, and clear risk framing before comparing stocks. This skill turns Longbridge data into Graham NCAV screens, Buffett moat reviews, and traceable investment research reports.
actual_capabilities: Routes single-stock and batch requests to Graham or Buffett analysis workflows.
Guides Longbridge CLI calls for financial statements, quotes, dividends, ownership, and peers.
Applies NCAV, PE, PB, dividend, debt coverage, and earnings-stability filters.
Scores Buffett-style moat, financial health, management, valuation, and long-term visibility.
Requires source appendices and reconciliation checks before scoring or ranking.
Produces multilingual reports in English, Simplified Chinese, or Traditional Chinese.
limitations: It is prompt-only and depends on available Longbridge CLI, MCP, or WebSearch data.
It does not execute trades or provide personalized financial advice.
Screening results can be stale if financial data or filings are delayed.
Qualitative moat judgments still require human review and independent verification.

use_cases.title: Screen Deep Value Candidates
use_cases.description: Run Graham-style filters across an index and return ranked candidates with NCAV, safety margin, and value-trap warnings.

use_cases.title: Review A Single Stock
use_cases.description: Create a source-backed stock diagnostic that reconciles financial statements before scoring value or quality factors.

use_cases.title: Build A Quality Watchlist
use_cases.description: Find Buffett-style compounders by combining hard financial filters with moat, management, valuation, and runway scoring.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Stock
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze AAPL.US with the Buffett moat framework. Use Longbridge data first, reconcile the figures, and include a source appendix.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Graham Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen the S&P 500 for Graham NCAV and defensive-investor candidates. Show the top results, exclusions, waiting-cost data, and value-trap warnings.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Frameworks
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare 00700.HK under Graham NCAV and Buffett moat methods. Explain which framework fits better and cite every financial figure.

prompt_templates.title: Customize A Buffett Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Find US consumer stocks with 5-year ROE above 18%, positive free cash flow, and reasonable valuation. Rank by moat quality, then valuation tier.

output_examples.output: A five-dimension moat report with star ratings, holding-period guidance, valuation tier, source appendix, and a clear non-advice disclaimer.

output_examples.input: Screen HSI.HK for Graham-style cigar-butt stocks.
output_examples.output: A ranked table of candidates with NCAV ratio, PE, PB, dividend yield, adjusted score, data exclusions, and next-step diagnostics.
Three to five candidate cards showing moat type, key financial highlights, valuation read, minimum holding period, and data-source rows.

best_practices: Provide a ticker, market, index, or sector so the workflow can select the right universe.
Review the data-source appendix before relying on scores or rankings.
Treat outputs as research support and verify conclusions against your own risk tolerance.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for short-term trading signals or market timing.
Do not treat a high score as a buy recommendation.
Do not ignore reconciliation failures or missing source rows.

faq.question: Does this skill provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides research structure and metric analysis for reference only.

faq.question: What data source does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers Longbridge CLI data, with MCP fallback and WebSearch only for genuine gaps.

faq.question: Can it screen many stocks at once?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Graham and Buffett batch screening across an index, sector, market, or watchlist.

faq.question: Why does it run reconciliation checks?
faq.answer: Reconciliation helps catch inconsistent financial statements before scores or rankings are shown.

faq.question: Which languages can reports use?
faq.answer: Reports can match English, Simplified Chinese, or Traditional Chinese user input.

faq.question: Does it require Longbridge login?
faq.answer: The skill metadata says public access is expected, but some CLI data may still require local setup.
